creator: andrews@TECHNOLOGIST.COM
text:
On Sun, May 07, 2000 at 01:30:03PM -0700, Ben Smithurst wrote:
>  It works fine for me.  If $file exists in the output directory, that
>  copy is used, otherwise the copy in /usr/local/share/bkmrkconv is used.

So three places to look (in order):
        1) argument given by -d (output directory).
        2) User's ~/.bkmrkconv/*
        3) Global /usr/local/share/bkmrkconv/.

Sound good?

-- 
Will Andrews <andrews@technologist.com>
